title: The MGR-Vijay parallel: ‘Jana Nayagan’ revives a rare moment in Tamil Nadu history
content: When MGR became Chief Minister, ‘Meenava Nanban’ followed. Now, history repeats with Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’, releasing on July 23

title: Post-mortem report of murdered man confirms multiple deep stab wounds
content: The report confirmed more than 40 wounds on the victim’s body which was recovered from a pool of blood on July 19

title: Associations to protest in Chennai on July 22 for unconditional implementation of 33% reservation for women
content: The protest would demand the implementation of 33% reservation for women without linking it to the Census and delimitation exercise

title: TVK government in Tamil Nadu faces a fiscal test in balancing populism and prudence
content: “We can have a welfare State only if we work for it and step up production,” said the then Chief Minister C. Rajagopalachari in June 1952

title: CJP protest in Delhi LIVE: CJP says 'brutal crackdown' underway on protesters at Jantar Mantar
content: CJP spokesperson met J.P. Nadda and submitted a letter with their demands; Sonam Wangchuk wrote to Safdarjung Hospital, saying he wanted to join Parliament March

title: Bhaktavatsalam’s take on the destruction of documents relating to anti-Hindi agitation in Tamil Nadu
content: He said he was advised that papers on politicians against whom detention orders had been served need not be kept so that they would not be available to the very people who were likely to take charge of the government. When Ministers came to know about officers who had made reports about them, they would get prejudiced, he further said

title: Meenambal Sivaraj: a trailblazer for Dalit rights and women empowerment
content: Meenambal Sivaraj was born on December 12, 1904, in Rangoon, in a family that placed great importance on education and service. One of her most significant contributions was her work with All India Scheduled Castes Federation founded by Ambedkar

title: The Kumbakonam connection of MGR, M.K. Nambyar and M.S. Swaminathan
content: The families of the iconic film star and former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, the distinguished constitutional lawyer, and the celebrated agricultural scientist hailed from Kerala. But they spent a formative part of their lives in Kumbakonam, known for its rich cultural heritage, before rising to national prominence in politics, cinema, law, and science

title: The 1995 caste reprisals that scarred southern T.N. also touched Communist leader Nallakannu’s life
content: On December 3, 1995, Nallakannu’s 84-year-old father-in-law, A.K. Annasamy, was murdered by a gang while he was asleep at his house in Marudanvalvu village, then part of Chidambaranar district (now Thoothukudi district)

title: Kerala power crisis: Regulatory panel urges KSEB to ‘improve’ electricity demand forecasts
content: Commission notes that any decision on power purchase based on incorrect demand and supply position is likely to result in operational challenges and financial liability that ultimately gets reflected in retail tariff of consumers

title: Congress slams Centre over handling Wangchuk, exam issues; opposes Higher Education Bill
content: The Congress leader alleged that despite repeated question paper leaks, Mr. Pradhan continued in office and remained “unperturbed,” while the protesters were demanding his resignation

title: Dolphin City: Chennai’s dolphinarium that shaped national debate on marine mammal captivity over 25 years ago
content: Hosting a dolphinarium in Chennai turned into controversy as all the three dolphins died, setting off one of the country’s earliest debates on the ethics of marine mammal captivity

title: When a 16-day strike by transport employees and a bus accident on Deepavali shook Tamil Nadu in 2001
content: In November 2001, transport corporation employees’ unions struck work demanding the Tamil Nadu government to increase annual bonus to 20%. During the strike, 28 persons were killed in Thoothukudi district in an accident involving a TNSTC bus allegedly driven by a temporary casual labourer.

title: NC protests in Delhi to demand statehood for J&K, says government's promise remains unfulfilled
content: Opposition PDP leader Iltija Mufti described the ruling NC’s protest as a “lacklustre and deliberately understated event” for not demanding the restoration of Article 370

title: Reassessing Periyar E.V. Ramasamy’s take on Tamil, which he called a barbarian language
content: The Dravidar Kazhagam founder believed that Tamil lacked sufficient literature that would pave the way for human development and cultivation of scientific temper. ‘It is my anguish that Tamil could not become a language of science and rationalism like English,’ he wrote while defending his characterisation of the language

title: J. Sivashanmugam Pillai: a tall leader of Tamil Nadu belonging to what was once known Depressed Classes
content: J. Sivashanmugam Pillai was the first Scheduled Caste member to become the Tamil Nadu Assembly Speaker in 1946. He was known for impartiality, with former CM Kamaraj saying, ‘Though a Congressman, he held the scales even.’ He demitted the office in 1955 to become Member of the UPSC and was elected to the Rajya Sabha in 1962

title: Udhagamandalam’s own Charing Cross and its London disconnect
content: It has long been the entrance to Udhagamandalam, the headquarters of The Nilgiris district. It shares its name with a junction in Westminster, London, where six routes meet. Hence, many believe that the Charing Cross in Udhagamandalam is modelled on the one in London

title: A Lok Sabha election defeat and dismissal of the MGR Government
content: Nearly a month later when there were talks in the corridors of power that the Indira Gandhi Government may dismiss governments in opposition-ruled States – citing the precedent set in 1977 by the Janata Party Government, MGR described it as “speculation”

title: Prohibitory orders in Mumbai from July 23 to August 6; cops cite likelihood of public order disturbance
content: The order will not cover gatherings at courts, government offices and local bodies for official work, academic activities in schools, colleges and educational institutions, and assemblies at factories, shops and establishments for normal trade and business

title: How ordinary people of Tamil Nadu participated in the making of the Constitution
content: A resident of Kattunedungulam village of the Sivaganga district, sent a hand-written note with a demand that members of his community should have freedom of worship and autonomy to manage their own community

title: A wildlife association that helped conserve the Nilgiris’ biodiversity
content: The NWEA came into existence in colonial India, when British colonists discovered a “hunters’ paradise” in the Nilgiris

title: M.G. Ramachandran’s hospitalisation in the U.S. and homecoming after treatment
content: MGR was flown to America on November 6, 1984, on a chartered Air India flight and was admitted to the Downstate Medical Centre in Brooklyn, New York. At the time of admission, he was not fully conscious after suffering a stroke. Later, he underwent a kidney transplant surgery at the hospital.

title: How Tamil Nadu’s two-language policy impacted the teachers of other languages in 1968
content: On January 23, 1968, the first DMK government, headed by C.N. Annadurai, adopted a resolution in the Madras Assembly scrapping the three-language formula and mandating the teaching of only Tamil and English. The immediate switchover affected over 4,000 Hindi and Sanskrit teachers at about 2,400 schools in the State

title: Documents on anti-Hindi agitation wiped out on order by Tamil Nadu’s last Congress CM Bhaktavatsalam
content: On February 24, 1967, a day after the Assembly election results were declared, the outgoing Chief Minister M. Bhaktavatsalam wrote a note to the Public Department. It said, “Records connected with the anti-Hindi agitation need not be kept. This may also apply to records maintained by the police.”

title: Why the Church of Scotland Mission was worried about schools in Madras falling into debt
content: While notable progress was made in the area of female education, there was also concern over the lack of availability of female teachers to tutor the girls.

title: Why Governor K.K. Shah recommended dismissal of DMK government in Tamil Nadu during Emergency in 1976
content: Not clamping down on anti-Emergency publications too led to the dissolution of Karunanidhi-led DMK government in Tamil Nadu in 1976, when the State was brought under the President’s rule for the fist time 50 years ago.

title: Ram Mandir donations theft row: Supreme Court suggests setting up a SIT
content: The Supreme Court said it will read the status report submitted by Uttar Pradesh and posted the case for further hearing; CJI Surya Kant objects to the multiplicity of petitions in the case

title: Capturing the cruelty of leprosy in a dark and merciless language
content: The anguish and alienation associated with leprosy have long made it a potent subject in literature, inspiring some of the modern writing in Tamil

title: How a political and legal battle halted efforts to conserve Tiruvannamalai temple 25 years ago
content: More than two decades ago, the Archaeological Survey of India proposed to declare the Arunachaleswara temple a “monument of national importance.” The proposal, however, was shelved after a row fomented by vested interests

title: Woman arrested for killing newborn granddaughter in Erode
content: Police said she allegedly strangled the newborn to death at a private hospital in Bhavani because the child was the family’s second daughter

title: When E.V.K. Sampath hit the headlines in Tamil Nadu
content: Sampath, nephew of E.V. Ramasamy, enjoyed a special relationship with DMK founder C.N. Annadurai. Before other DMK leaders, he realised the futility of pursuing the demand for a Dravida Nadu. He quit the DMK in 1961 and founded a party, which he merged with the Congress. Sampath’s birth centenary falls on March 6, 2026.

title: Vadasery’s temple jewellery: a craft caught between heritage and high gold, silver prices
content: Temple jewellery continues to survive with its distinctive manufacturing method and rich antiquity. But the artisans of Vadasery in Nagercoil, who practise the craft, are buffeted by the drop in demand with gold and silver prices going up

title: How V.O. Chidambaram Pillai earned the title  ‘Sekkizhutha Chemmal’
content: VOC was arrested on the charge of sedition on March 12, 1908. A British jailer noticed his hands bleeding from the harsh labour of separating coir and spinning rope in the Coimbatore prison. Hence, he advised him to work at the oil press instead. VOC  accepted the suggestion and pulled the oil press himself, a task normally reserved for bulls

title: On the approach and outlook of Tamil Nadu’s Scheduled Caste leaders in late 1940s
content: In the 1946 elections to various provinces, the Congress demonstrated its superiority over the Scheduled Castes Federation (SCF) founded by B.R. Ambedkar. In the erstwhile Madras Presidency, the party bagged 27 out of 30 seats allotted to SCs.

title: 170 years ago, a debate in Britain parliament on torture in Madras
content: Forms of torture in Madras Presidency at the time included tying a man by the hair of his head to the tail of an ass, and parading him though the public market

title: Chennai’s iconic annual fair at Island Grounds turns 50
content: The 70-day-long India Tourism and Industrial Fair that began at the Island Grounds in the city recently turned 50 this year. The Sutrula Porutkatchi was conceived and named by former Chief Minister M. Karunanidhi. It began in the city in 1974 as the ‘Pongal Tourist Trade Fair.’

title: Ram Setu vs. Sethusamudram project: When Tamil Nadu BJP headquarters was attacked by DMK cadres
content: In response to DMK president M. Karunanidhi’s remark on Ram Setu, Vishwa Hindu Parishad (VHP) leader and former BJP MP Ram Vilas Vedanti triggered a major controversy in September 2007 by saying that “whosoever chopped off the head and tongue of the DMK president would be gifted in gold.”

title: How the Tamil Nadu legislature, in the late 1940s, viewed the making of the Constitution
content: In the Constituent Assembly’s debates (1946-49), K. Santhanam and N.G. Ranga, both from southern States representing the present Tamil Nadu and Andhra Pradesh, had strongly argued against any attempt to make the Central government more powerful under the garb of expanding the Concurrent List

title: When eggs, stones were hurled at T.N. Governor Channa Reddy’s convoy and CM Jayalalithaa sought his recall
content: The incident occurred in 1995 when hostilities between Jayalalithaa and Reddy were common knowledge. While the Governor sent a report to the Centre against the AIADMK government, the Chief Minister wrote to President Shankar Dayal Sharma and Prime Minister P.V. Narasimha Rao.

title: Tamil Nadu’s link to a key amendment to the Constitution on freedom of press, expression
content: On March 1, 1950, the Madras government banned Cross Roads, a Left-leaning journal. A month later, the Supreme Court’s full Bench, by five to one, quashed the order. The Constitution had to be amended in the light of the landmark judgment. Parliament adopted the amendment Bill, with 246 members voting in favour and 14 against

title: Six decades of Blue Cross: from humble beginnings to setting the standard for animal welfare
content: In 1959, Captain V. Sundaram brought home two stranded puppies he found on a flooded street near his house at T. Nagar. He built makeshift kennels at home for injured and abandoned animals. This organisation was registered as the BCI in 1964

title: An idol found near Gangaikonda Cholapuram confirms the ‘Thiruvarasu’ of Nathamunigal
content: Near the Gangaikonda Cholapuram-Kumbakonam Road is a place called Sorgapallam or Sembodai where an idol of Srinivasa Perumal as once found half-buried under a tree. In the year 2000, another idol was found buried in a field. It was that of the first of the Sri Vaishnava Acharyas, who was said to have been at the place in his final hours

title: PM Modi urges constructive parliamentary debate
content: The Prime Minister highlights recent milestones during the customary address to mediapersons ahead of the beginning of the Parliament session

title: Mahatma Gandhi, his Salem visit, and philately
content: The philatelic museum, which was set up in January 1997, exhibits commemorative stamps and first-day postal covers issued by India and a host of countries, including those in the African and Caribbean regions on Gandhiji. It also showcases a reclining teakwood chair used by Gandhiji during his stay in Salem

title: Demographic shifts and the rise of Hindutva in Kanniyakumari district
content: Kanniyakumari holds a distinctive place in Tamil Nadu’s political history. Despite nearly six decades of Dravidian dominance in the State, it became the first district to elect a Hindutva-backed candidate as early as 1989, when V. Balachandram of Hindu Desam won the Padmanabhapuram constituency

title: Delhi riots case: Supreme Court stays HC order granting Pinjra Tod activist Devangana Kalita access to probe documents
content: According to the Delhi Police, Ms. Kalita and several other activists had conspired to trigger unrest in northeast Delhi in 2020 under the guise of peaceful protests

title: How Tamil Nadu waited for Assembly election results over the decades
content: For the first time in Tamil Nadu’s history, the Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) were deployed in all the 234 Assembly segments in the 2001 Assembly elections, mirroring simultaneous rollouts in Kerala, Puducherry, and West Bengal

title: Ontavantha Pidariyum Oor Pidariyum: The story behind the proverb
content: The proverb’s origins are traced to Udayalur in Thanjavur district, according to noted epigraphist T.N. Subramanian

title: Party support for Independent candidate, an election strategy in which Jayalalithaa succeeded and MGR failed
content: Tamil Nadu has in the past witnessed two elections in which Independent candidates secured the backing of a mainstream political party — with strikingly different results. The two instances were separated by nearly 25 years

title: CM land purchase allegations: Madhya Pradesh Government introduces UCC Bill amid Congress protests
content: A recent newspaper report had claimed that Madhya Pradesh CM Mohan Yadav and his family members purchased huge land parcels after the latter became Chief Minister of the State

title: Of MGR, Sivaji Ganesan, and Tamil Nadu’s star-studded politics
content: Actors with huge fan followings, such as Sivaji Ganesan, Kamal Haasan, and Vijayakant, as well as popular stars like K. Bhagyaraj, Karthik, and Ramarajan, have tried their luck in elections with limited success

title: Thanjavur Big Temple inscriptions bring alive the fierce fight between Shivaji and Afzal Khan
content: The inscriptions, written in Marathi on the south-western walls near the Vinayagar Temple, also describe the history of the Maratha kings and praise Danish missionary Rev. Friedrich Christian Schwartz. They also highlight how Serfoji sent his army and offered financial assistance to the British during the war against the Marudu brothers

title: Salem C. Vijiaraghavachariar, the freedom fighter who fought against Kala Paani
content: Salem C. Vijiaraghavachariar, one of the doyens of the national movement during the pre-Gandhi era and the first Tamil to become the All India Congress Committee president, was ordered to be transported to Andamans to serve a 10-year imprisonment. He appealed to the High Court of Madras and got his conviction quashed on January 9, 1883

title: How Scheduled Castes in Tirunelveli empowered themselves by establishing schools
content: The community established schools not only in Tirunelveli district but also in other parts of Tamil Nadu. Now, there are 28 schools originally established by the community that are functioning as government-aided primary schools in Tirunelveli

title: Chief Ministers of Tamil Nadu who were defeated in elections
content: From the early years of Dravidian politics to more recent decades, voters have not hesitated to defeat stalwarts, even Chief Ministers

title: India records 78 doping cases till July 15; Uttar Pradesh, Punjab top list: Sports Minister Mandaviya
content: Manuskh Mandaviya, in a written response, said 78 Adverse Analytical Findings have been recorded so far in 2026

title: Acts committed as juvenile cannot justify preventive detention after attaining majority: J&K High Court
content: Detenue could not have been detained under preventive laws for the activities alleged committed by him at the time when he was a juvenile, the court says

title: Rajya Sabha MP Manoj Jha writes to PM Modi; seeks halt to demolition of Mohammad Ali Jauhar University buildings
content: Manoj Jha described the proposed demolition as "grossly excessive" and questioned its proportionality, alleging that it appears to be politically motivated

title: CJP leaders handover 3 demands to JP Nadda; Shah meets Dharmendra
content: Cockroach Janata Party leaders met Union minister JP Nadda regarding the NEET-UG paper leak case. Protesters marched towards Parliament demanding accountability and specific government actions. The Union minister sought time to consult leadership on the party's demands. Meanwhile, Union Home Minister Amit Shah met the Education Minister at Parliament. Police used mild force and tear gas as demonstrators attempted to breach barricades.

title: Messi branded a ‘baby’ after desperate attempt to get Cucurella sent off
content: Messi’s World Cup final frustration sparked controversy after he appealed for action against Marc Cucurella’s mouth-covering exchange during Argentina’s defeat to Spain. While no punishment followed, pundits criticised the reaction as unsportsmanlike. Spain ultimately prevailed through Ferran Torres’ extra-time winner, ending Argentina’s title defence and leaving Messi’s final World Cup appearance marked by disappointment.

title: JPMorgan made 40% AI job cuts in some teams, but CEO is not ‘convinced’ of its results
content: JPMorgan Chase has reduced some jobs by up to forty percent using artificial intelligence.  CEO Jamie Dimon believes AI offers no unique competitive advantage for the bank.  He stated that AI will not significantly increase the bank's profit margins.  The bank is increasing recruitment for AI-related roles while hiring fewer bankers.  JPMorgan Chase reported strong net income and investment banking fees for the second quarter.

title: From Tejas rejection to 5th-gen fighter jet hopes: Can Kaveri 2.0 finally deliver?
content: India's Kaveri jet engine project shows signs of revival after years of delays. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh urged scientists to develop sixth-generation fighter engines soon. Technologies from the Kaveri program will support future combat aircraft and unmanned vehicles. India continues to rely on imported GE engines for its current fighter fleet. This renewed focus aims for self-reliance in military aviation propulsion systems.

title: 'It was written': Pedri's cryptic 13-month-old post goes viral after Spain's WC win
content: Spain midfielder Pedri celebrated their FIFA World Cup 2026 victory with a mysterious social media post.  He had shared a cryptic number sequence over a year prior to the triumph.  This sequence was interpreted by fans as the date of the final match.  Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 after extra time to secure their second title.  Pedri played a vital role throughout the team's successful campaign.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: Paid for 90g detergent, got only 70g; consumer wins Rs 50,000
content: Hindustan Unilever Limited was found liable for selling an underweight Surf Excel detergent pouch. The state consumer commission reduced the compensation awarded to the original complainant. The case began in 2020 when a consumer found a packet lighter than labelled. HUL's appeal argued against the district commission's jurisdiction and findings. The commission upheld the unfair trade practice finding but lowered the financial award.

title: Google Maps pit turns out to be 390-million-year-old meteorite crater
content: An amateur astronomer found a circular depression on a map.  Scientists investigated and confirmed it as a meteorite impact crater.  This ancient crater is approximately 390 million years old.  The discovery adds Uhackatik to Canada's list of impact structures.  Modern tools enable everyday people to contribute to scientific understanding.

title: How the Sun ‘goes to sleep’ may reveal future space weather risks
content: Scientists have developed a new method to predict solar cycle strength.  This approach identifies a precise point where extreme space weather abruptly ceases.  Researchers can now forecast the next solar cycle's activity up to seven years in advance.  Early predictions suggest Solar Cycle 26 may be moderate in strength.  Further refinement of these forecasts will occur in approximately two years.

title: Europe found a clever new job for dead wind turbines, and the US is copying it
content: Wind turbine blades are notoriously hard to recycle, giving critics of wind energy an easy line of attack. But an idea born in Ireland in 2019 is quietly fixing that. Decommissioned fiberglass blades are being turned into girders for pedestrian bridges—first in Cork, then near Atlanta by 2025—swapping out steel and giving retired blades a useful second life instead of a landfill.

title: Vacant for decades? Why the government can't simply take over abandoned properties
content: In India, the absence of a comprehensive law governing abandoned buildings leads to fragmented regulations across various legislative acts. Municipal authorities hold the authority to inspect and classify buildings as dangerous. Owners are notified to undertake necessary repairs or secure their properties, with demolition a potential option in cases of imminent risk. Both Maharashtra and Delhi enforce mandatory structural evaluations for older buildings.

title: Colby Covington targets ex-UFC middleweight champ after Belal Muhammad clash
content: The proposed wrestling match between Colby Covington and Belal Muhammad is no longer happening. After weeks of speculation following their heated confrontation at Real American Freestyle (RAF) 11, Covington has confirmed that the matchup has been called off. Speaking to Submission Radio, the former UFC interim welterweight champion revealed that he is now looking at different opponents for his next appearance.

title: 'Don't arm-twist the government': Kangana Ranaut criticises CJP protest near Parliament
content: Kangana Ranaut criticized ongoing CJP protests near Parliament, stating demonstrations should not pressure the government. She believes Parliament is the proper forum for discussing public issues and government decisions. Protests causing disruptions are inappropriate as the elected government has a mandate to govern. Meanwhile, CJP protesters faced heavy security and tear gas as they marched towards Parliament. Several Bollywood personalities have voiced support for Sonam Wangchuk's ongoing hunger strike.

title: TheMightyBA's Streamer University 2026 controversy explained
content: The controversy at Streamer University 2026 surrounding TheMightyBA intensified when footage surfaced of him patting MeesterKeem's head, triggering a storm of criticism. Ludwig stood by TheMightyBA, suggesting that redemption is possible, drawing parallels to IShowSpeed’s remarkable evolution. With persistent effort and time, creators can address their past errors and forge a path to redemption.

title: Indigenous bullet trains: What’s happening on country's first high-speed rail project
content: India is planning to begin trials of its domestically developed bullet trains by the middle of 2027. Civil construction on the 97-km Surat-Vapi high-speed rail stretch is scheduled to be completed this year.

title: Karnataka HC questions 12-year delay in education quota for transgender persons
content: Karnataka High Court questioned the state government for its delayed implementation of transgender reservation in educational institutions. A transgender student's admission denial to NLSIU Bangalore initiated the legal proceedings for this reservation policy. The court emphasized the Supreme Court's 2014 NALSA ruling, which mandated such reservations for transgender individuals. Karnataka already provides reservation in government jobs, making the education sector's delay questionable.

title: Locked flat, confession voicemail: Thane couple booked for murdering woman’s lover
content: A decomposed body was found in a locked Dombivli flat after neighbours noticed a foul smell. The tenant's wife allegedly confessed to the murder via voicemail sent to the house owner. She claimed her lover had been troubling her, leading to the killing by the couple. Police are searching for the absconding couple after registering a murder case.

title: Era of cheap borrowing may be over as world enters 'a new macro regime': Moody's
content: The era of ultra-low interest rates is ending, ushering in higher borrowing costs.  Investors anticipate sustained investment in artificial intelligence and infrastructure projects.  Geopolitical risks are also expected to remain elevated in the coming years.  Bond markets reflect expectations of structurally higher interest rates for an extended period.  Capital will likely flow towards AI, defense, and critical minerals sectors.

title: 'Lacklustre': PDP takes swipe at NC's Delhi protest for J&K statehood
content: Peoples Democratic Party leader Iltija Mufti criticized the National Conference protest for statehood restoration. She deemed the demonstration lacklustre and deliberately understated, lacking a call for Article 370. Mufti argued a Jantar Mantar protest would have demanded special status reinstatement. The PDP stayed away, insisting on Article 370 restoration and prisoner release. This follows the 2019 revocation of Article 370 and state bifurcation.

title: Away from the bell and egg-and-bacon ties, Rohit keeps his England tradition alive
content: In the lead-up to the third ODI between India and England at Lord's, more focus was on Rohit Sharma than the series decider itself. With India's ODI World Cup plans underway, Rohit's form came under fire, and retirement rumours gathered momentum. With Virat Kohli batting at the other end in a monstrous 388-run chase, he decided to shut them down by becoming the first Indian to score a ton at the Home of Cricket.

title: India approves its first dengue vaccine QDENGA after DCGI clearance
content: Dengue is a mosquito-borne viral disease reported in more than 125 countries. According to the company, India accounts for nearly one-third of the global dengue burden, with rapid urbanisation, climate change and widespread mosquito breeding continuing to increase the risk of infection across the country. Vaccine QDENGA is given as two injections of 0.5 ml, administered under the skin three months apart.

title: 'Dhamaal 4' BO Day 11 [LIVE]: Ajay Devgn's comedy reaches Rs 125 crore India net milestone
content: Ajay Devgn's 'Dhamaal 4' continues its impressive second weekend run at the Indian box office. After comfortably crossing the Rs 100 crore India net mark on Day 8 and registering a strong Saturday jump on Day 9, the comedy entertainer has now entered Day 10 with Rs 112.46 crore India net (live). The Indra Kumar directorial is now targeting a Rs 115 crore India net finish by the end of its second weekend, while the worldwide gross is expected to move closer to the Rs 160 crore milestone, despite competition from Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey'.

title: Dealer sold ‘test-drive’ car as brand new; buyer wins over Rs 9.2 lakh payout
content: The NCDRC ruled Tata Motors is not liable for a dealer selling a demo car as new.  The buyer purchased a Tata Manza Elan which had been used for eight months.  The district commission ordered the dealer to refund the full amount and pay compensation.  The state commission had made Tata Motors jointly liable with the dealer.  The NCDRC restored the district commission's order, absolving the manufacturer and restoring compensation.

title: Buying a corner flat? Pros, cons and key factors to consider
content: Corner flats frequently boast enhanced natural illumination and superior airflow for their occupants. These residences often present expansive vistas and adaptable floor plans. That said, the downside includes a steeper price tag compared to more centrally located units, along with increased vulnerability to the elements and potential noise disruptions. Buyers need to evaluate these advantages and drawbacks thoroughly prior to finalizing their choice.

title: NTA names 5 NEET candidates, says OMR claims built on digital forgeries
content: The National Testing Agency has dismissed allegations regarding discrepancies in OMR sheets presented by five NEET-UG candidates. They emphasized that the images circulating are likely digitally altered, enhanced with AI, or otherwise modified. The agency affirmed the authenticity of the OMR sheets and the accuracy of the candidates' scores. Severe penalties may be imposed for submitting fraudulent records under new regulations.

title: Tech haze: IT cos revisit campus hiring playbook, defer onboarding
content: Campus offers are now being delayed and roles are quietly withdrawn by IT firms.  Joining dates are increasingly tied to shifting business demand and administrative issues.  Fresh graduates face uncertainty as companies cite business requirements and complex training needs.  The IT industry's hiring model is undergoing a structural shift towards specialized skills.  This trend indicates a flattening of the traditional pyramid hiring model for graduates.

title: Will Lionel Messi retire? Argentina coach breaks silence after World Cup final defeat
content: Spain secured their second FIFA World Cup title after defeating Argentina in the 2026 final. Substitute Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal in extra time, ending Argentina's reign. Lionel Messi's future remains his personal decision, according to coach Lionel Scaloni. Spain completed an unbeaten tournament, conceding only one goal throughout their campaign. This victory marked Spain's first World Cup win since their triumph in 2010.

title: After Skyroot, more private firms aim to take commercial dreams to sky
content: India's private space sector is accelerating after a historic rocket launch.  Companies are now preparing for commercial missions and reusable rocket development.  Several spacetech startups are planning multiple satellite launches in coming years.  This increased launch activity will help Indian satellite firms compete globally.  The growth unlocks new opportunities for defense and private sector applications.

title: R Madhavan shares an inspirational quote about life
content: Actor R Madhavan stars in the upcoming biopic 'G.D.N.' about inventor G. D. Naidu. The film is scheduled for release on August 7, 2026, and is generating significant buzz. Madhavan recently shared an inspirational quote emphasizing kindness and humility over external achievements. He believes true life fulfillment comes from being real and humane towards others. The movie also features Priyamani and Sathyaraj in key roles.

title: After cars, now ethanol may make its way to your kitchen
content: As part of the proposed framework, the government is expected to examine subsidy mechanisms and measures to develop the necessary supply chain for wider adoption of ethanol.

title: 'Feeling very fine': Wangchuk seeks discharge from hospital to join 'Chalo Sansad' march
content: Sonam Wangchuk feels well and seeks temporary hospital release to join a protest march. His supporters question the continued restrictions on his movement and communication. Wangchuk had previously threatened to end his hunger strike if demands were met. Protesters clashed with police near Jantar Mantar while attempting to reach Parliament. Wangchuk was hospitalized after his health deteriorated during his hunger strike.

title: 'Let the noise be there': Rohit Sharma brushes aside retirement talk after Lord's ton
content: Rohit Sharma silenced retirement rumors with a century against England at Lord's. He scored 138 runs, nearly guiding India to a record chase. Sharma stated his focus remains on contributing to the team's success. He pays no attention to outside noise and its presence. His performance keeps him in contention for future matches.

title: 'Not talking about some 56-year-old youth': PM Modi takes apparent swipe at Rahul Gandhi
content: Prime Minister Modi lauded India's young space innovators while addressing Parliament. He highlighted Skyroot Aerospace's average team age of twenty-eight years. This achievement marks a significant milestone for India's private space sector. The Prime Minister also discussed India's economic resilience amidst global challenges. He appealed for productive discussions during the Monsoon Session of Parliament.

title: Lionel Messi exits FIFA World Cup in tears and as football's greatest
content: Messi, head bowed, hands on his hips, watched 19-year-old Lamine Yamal embrace Torres after the winning goal. Moments later, with tears running down his cheeks, he collected his runners-up medal while Spain celebrated amid confetti and fireworks.

title: Lockheed to build low-cost Patriot interceptor using Hit-to-Kill technology - how it works
content: Lockheed Martin introduced a new, cheaper interceptor for the Patriot air defence system. This PAC-3 ACE missile costs less than half of current options. It aims to help allies counter growing drone and missile threats affordably. Initial production could begin within thirty-six months with industry partners. This development addresses rising global conflicts and competition from new weapon makers.

title: Kerala court sentences 61-year-old to death in Nenmara double murder case
content: The quantum of punishment was decided after the sentencing hearing concluded last Tuesday before Judge Kenneth George, who heard detailed submissions from both the prosecution and the defence before reserving the verdict. The prosecution urged the court to impose the death penalty, arguing that the crime fell within the "rarest of rare" category. It told the court that Chenthamara posed a continuing threat to society and cited his remarks after conviction to argue that he had shown no remorse.

title: 'It's all out in the media': Gill denies Rohit Sharma retirement talks
content: Amid speculation over his ODI future, Rohit entered the record books by becoming the first Indian to score an ODI century at Lord's. The 39-year-old made 138 off 110 balls in what was his 19th year in ODI cricket.

title: Drunk Man Strangles Wife To Death After Fight Over Dinner In Gurugram: Police
content: The accused had initially claimed his wife died from excessive alcohol consumption, but the post-mortem revealed she had been strangled to death.

title: Tear Gas, Lathicharge As At Least 20,000 March With CJP To Parliament
content: CJP Parliament March: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) supporters had announced a "Chalo Sansad" march against alleged irregularities in NEET.

title: Spain's World Cup Win Celebrations Turn Trafic As Teen Dies In Fountain Collapse
content: Overcrowded fountain collapses during midnight soccer celebrations, killing a 13-year-old boy and injuring several others.

title: Over 1.7 Lakh People Affected In Assam Floods, Army Launches 'Operation Jal Rahat'
content: Floods in Assam have affected over 1.7 lakh people across 12 districts, 30 revenue circles and 580 villages.

title: DMK MLA Arrested Over Defamatory, Threatening Remarks Against Vijay
content: The controversy stems from a public meeting held on July 18, where Markendiyan responded to the Chief Minister's remarks about his "search for father".

title: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: Dharmendra Pradhan, Amit Shah Meet In Parliament Amid CJP Protest March
content: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: The police had denied the Cockroach Janta Party the permission to hold the protest march and banned large gatherings.

title: Fear Of Rising Disasters Push Uttarakhand To Revive Chipko Movement
content: The chainsaws have gone silent in the Uttarakhand hills after the government cracked under pressure from a fierce, citizen-led eco-rebellion.

title: Inside The Secret CIA Mission That Cleared UAE's Path To Advanced AI Chips
content: Jonny Gannon's mission reportedly went beyond intelligence gathering and became part of a larger effort to reshape US-UAE AI ties.

title: Tripura Top Cop Found Dead In Office, Cops Suspect He Shot Himself
content: According to preliminary information, Dhankar is believed to have shot himself with his own service pistol.

title: 8th Pay Commission: What Is Pay Scale Merger, Why Do Employee Unions Want It?
content: The 8th Pay Commission is expected to recommend changes that will affect around 50 lakh central government employees and nearly 65 lakh pensioners.

title: PM Modi's Dig At '56-Year-Old Youth' Rahul Gandhi Ahead Of Monsoon Session
content: PM Modi, while praising the Hyderabad-based Skyroot Aerospace for successfully launching Vikram-1, the country's first privately developed orbital rocket, said on Monday that the feat was achieved by youngsters with an average age of 28 years and not by a "56-year-old youth".

title: Trump Mum On Iran Plans As US Military Death Count Rises To 17 In War
content: Secret B-2 bomber videos and covert tanker movements hint at a major looming escalation while Trump publicly stalls.

title: 'Waiting For Their Arrival': Iran's Big Warning Against US Ground Invasion
content: US President Trump has repeatedly mused about ramping up American efforts, including potentially attempting to seize Kharg Island.

title: India's Battery Boom Faces A New Test: Who Will Control Scrap Supply?
content: In FY24, India imported nearly 1,53,500 tonnes of lead scrap worth around Rs 2,486 crore, mainly from the United States, the UK and Australia

title: How Courts Deal With Hunger Strikes: Sonam Wangchuk's Case Revives Debate
content: Sonam Wangchuk's case revives a longstanding legal debate over the limits of State intervention in hunger strikes and how courts have balanced personal autonomy with the duty to preserve life.

title: 9 Illegal Guns, 10 Arrests: Telangana Cops Crack Down Against Illegal Hunting
content: Police arrest 10 and seize nine illegal shotguns during crackdown after leopard death in Telangana forest

title: Indian Envoy Meets 'Distressed' Women Workers In Oman, Assures Help
content: The embassy's outreach comes amid recurring reports of abuse, exploitation and non-payment of wages involving Indian women domestic workers in Oman and other Gulf countries.

title: Beyond Low Cost: Why Foreigners Choose India's Healthcare Over The West
content: India's medical tourism industry is estimated to be worth $12.32 billion in 2026. It is growing much faster than the country's overall tourism sector.
